wiki_pages.title: Creating your LinkedIn profile Copy
wiki_pages.body_html:
Create your LinkedINprofile

Slides with steps in creating the profile

  •  Please use the LinkedIN checklist to get your LinkedIN profile created
  • You are creating the URL and will continue to work on it 
  • Connect with others at AnnieCannons
  • Follow at least one group of interest that is related to your field
  • Submit your LinkedIN url


LinkedIn profile creation checklist

  • Do you have the perfect profile photo (headshot, smiling, right attire)?
  • Does your headline match your desired job title?
  • Does your summary include both keywords + accomplishments?
  • Does your work experience include your resume bullets?
  • Does your education include your accomplishments outside of class?
  • Do your skills match the desired skills on your dream job description?
  • Do you have at least one recommendation?


Additional resources

The attached document offers  more ways to leverage your LinkedIN for your JOB search LinkedIn  for Tech Professionals Document

For those that want the extra challenge and further documentation checkout: All-Star Status and Strategic Networking on LinkedIn

wiki_pages.title: Directions: Completing the Google Workspace Course
wiki_pages.body_html:
Getting Started with Google Workspace

Welcome to the Pluralsight course created by Google Cloud.

Many companies use the Google Workspace suite of apps to organize and communicate. Understanding the ins and outs of these applications will help you be a valuable contributor to your teams. You can also include that you completed this Pluralsight course on your resume.

We welcome you to embrace learning as you go through the modules, rather than just an item to be checked off your to-do list. 

  • How would you approach this if your new boss at your dream job asked you to complete the course?
  • How can you invite yourself to slow down or take a deep breath if things feel boring or overwhelming?
  • If there are things that don’t match your learning style, how can you strategize or modify them? (Feel free to reach out if you need suggestions)
  • Access the course here: Getting Started with Google Workspace

Please note:

  • This assignment is required to continue to Intro to Programming (ITP). If you are going to miss the deadline (Thursday night), please make sure to submit your explanation.
    • You must either turn in the completed assignment or the late submission explanation by the due date.
  • Because tech is always changing, there may be things that have changed since the course was published. Please google or ask questions; we are here to support you.
  • If the instructions ask you to share something with a colleague, you can use your instructor’s email address. 

 

Assignments

Watch each video and complete each activity. You do not need to do the Business Exercise for any module.

We strongly recommend you complete the course in this order. That's because on occasion, you'll use skills you've already learned to complete the rest of the course. For example, we may ask you to place multiple screenshots in a Google Doc to turn in. 

It looks like SO. MUCH. WORK.

Don't panic! Each exercise will take you 5-15 minutes to complete, from watching the (short) video segments to completing the exercise. You should have plenty of time to complete the course, but if you are having trouble, be sure to reach out to your instructor as soon as possible.

Docs

  • Activity 1: Create and format a google doc
  • Activity 2: Name, star and save a google doc as a PDF
  • Activity 3: Insert images in a google doc
  • Activity 4: Use versions and shareable links
  • Activity 5: Download in different formats
  • Activity 6: Use comments and assign action items

 

Drive

  • Activity 1: Explore Google Drive
  • Activity 2: Understand collaborator roles
  • Activity 3: Create a new folder and add files
  • Activity 4: Work with folders in my drive
  • Activity 5: Work with files and folders in a shared drive
  • Activity 6: Create a google doc from a template

 

Gmail

  • Activity 1: Compose an email
  • Activity 2: Create and use labels
  • Activity 3: Create an event from gmail and view it in Calendar
  • Activity 4: Create a contact and a personal group
  • Activity 5: Create a new email signature
  • Activity 6: Create a mail template
  • Activity 7: Enable Out of Office

 

Calendar

  • Activity 1: Create and modify an event
  • Activity 2: Add a guest and attach a file from Google Drive
  • Activity 3: Use Google Calendar Search
  • Activity 4: Mark yourself Out Of Office and set a Reminder
  • Activity 5: Create and share a calendar

 

Sheets

  • Activity 1: Import an Excel Spreadsheet to Google Sheets
  • Activity 2: Manage Data in Google Sheets
  • Activity 3: Apply formatting to a Google Sheet
  • Activity 4: Use formulas to perform calculations
  • Activity 5: Use named ranges
  • Activity 6: Create a chart
  • Activity 7: Explore sharing options
  • Activity 8: Comments and action items

wiki_pages.title: Tech Foundations Study Module
wiki_pages.body_html: This page will contain a link to Jen's Storyline module. It will be on this page no later than Aug 18.

wiki_pages.title: Week 1 Overview
wiki_pages.body_html:
Welcome to AnnieCannons!

By the end of the week, you will: 

  • Meet instructors, staff, the Director of Learning Design and Technology, the Career Development Manager (CDM), and the Student Success Coordinator (SSC)
  • Understand the roadmap of learning at AnnieCannons
  • Set up your computer
  • Set up accounts for learning platforms, Slack, and Notion
  • Cultivate a growth mindset

wiki_pages.title: Week 2 Overview
wiki_pages.body_html:
By the end of the week, you will be able to:

  • Understand portfolios, resumes, LinkedIn, and the job process
  • Research a tech company
  • Create a presentation with Canva
  • Get into the Coding Mindset
  • Master study skills
  • Better manage PTSD/PTSS
  • Manage frustration tolerance

wiki_pages.title: Week 3 Overview
wiki_pages.body_html:
By the end of the week, you will be able to

  • Understand how to study with study skills
  • Understand how Canva works and use it to create graphics
  • Get an introduction to coding, HTML, and CodePen
  • Get an overview of technology in Tech Foundations

wiki_pages.title: Week 4 Overview
wiki_pages.body_html:
By the end of the week, you will be able to:

  • Complete the Google Workspace course from Pluralsight
  • Meet and get to know:
    • Robyn, Student Success Manager
    • Dawn, Career Development Manager
    • Arianna, Instructor
    • Haniya, Instructor

What to do this week

You will be working independently this week, completing a Pluralsight course on Google Workspace. You will also have individual meetings with Robyn, Dawn, and the instructor(s).

You are not required to be on Zoom until Thursday, Block C, other than for your meetings.

There are assignments to complete. You are expected to complete the workspace course and all assignments by the end of this week.

    • If you complete everything early, you may take the rest of the week off until Thursday, Block C.
    • If you have not completed everything by the end of Thursday Block B, you may work through the weekend to complete it.
    • You have not completed TCF until the Google Workspace course and its assignments are completed.

 

Your Daily Schedule

Tuesday, Block A

Report to class on Zoom as usual. Your instructor will explain the schedule to you.

Make an appointment with the Career Development Manager, Dawn: https://calendar.app.google/5fkcvkkpXfqXks4d8 

Make an appointment with the Student Success Manager, Robyn: https://calendar.app.google/cyptegziRQzyD6JR7 

Make an appointment with your instructor: [INSERT CALENDAR LINK HERE]

You may then begin work on the Google Workspace course.

Tuesday, Blocks B-D
Wednesday, Blocks A-D
Thursday, Blocks A-B

You are not required to be on Zoom.

Attend your meetings as scheduled with your instructor(s), Robyn, and Dawn.

Work on the Pluralsight course.

By the end of Block A on Wednesday and Thursday: 

Post a Slack update in the following format:

Yesterday I worked on ________________________. 

Today I will work on _________________________.

I am having trouble with X concept and need help. I have googled ______ to try to find the answer.

Or 

I am doing fine. There are no blockers to finish my work.

By the end of today, I estimate I will complete ________ videos and ____________ assignment(s).

(You may report on anything else after this - like any scheduled meeting(s) that day, for example.)

Thursday, Block C

You will be on Zoom. This time will be spent blogging about the week.

Thursday, Block D

You will be on Zoom. This is graduation time!

wiki_pages.title: Computer Literacy and Basic Computer Skills for Productivity
wiki_pages.body_html:
Computer Literacy & Basic Computer Skills for Productivity Course

This course offers several topics which are divided into different sections, including An Introduction To Computers, Microsoft Windows, & the Apple Mac. Students are encouraged to take the sections that focus on Mac & will be used more in this class. 

Mac Basics (15 mins) and Shortcut Keys (28 mins) are good sections to watch and practice. 

Note: Shortcut Keys Section uses a windows keyboard so some short cuts will be different. Windows uses control key while Mac uses command key.
wiki_pages.manifest_title: Computer Literacy and Basic Computer Skills for Productivity

wiki_pages.title: How to Access Google Workspace Courses
wiki_pages.body_html:
For Docs, Drive, and Gmail Courses:

  1. Go to Google Workspace Training
  2. Click "Log in" in the top right
  3. Sign in with your Google account
  4. You are required to do the following courses. The rest are optional if you have extra time:
    1. Google Workspace: Docs - Part 1
    2. Google Workspace: Docs - Part 2
    3. Google Workspace: Drive
    4. Google Workspace: Gmail

 

For Calendar Course:

  1. Go to Google Workspace for Beginners
  2. Click "Log in" in the top right
  3. Sign in with your Google account
  4. You are required to do the following courses. The rest are optional if you have extra time:
    1. Google Calendar for Beginners
wiki_pages.manifest_title: How to Access Google Workspace Courses

wiki_pages.title: Canvas code snippets for instructors
wiki_pages.body_html:
After clicking Edit in the upper right corner of the editing window, in the lower right corner of the editing window, you will see a </> icon. Clicking this will put you into the HTML for each page or assignment. You may edit the HTML directly, or you may incorporate inline styles for the document.

Below are some code snippets I've used in this course. Copy the snips from this page - do not copy from source code, as it will convert brackets to > and < symbols.

Late Assignment snippet

Using the HTML <details> element, we can create an accordion panel without JavaScript!

<p> </p>
<details style="padding: 0.5rem; border-radius: 5px; background-color: #eff; border: 1px solid #cdd;">
    <summary style="font-size: 1.5rem;">Late assignments/ Incomplete Assignments</summary>
    <p><span>In the case of incomplete assignments, please note that it is crucial to provide as much information as possible. This will help us understand why you were unable to meet the deadline and how we can best support you in completing the work.</span></p>
    <p><span>In the comments for the assignment, please include the following:</span></p>
    <ol>
        <li aria-level="1"><span>Why the assignment is late</span></li>
        <li aria-level="1"><span>What you still need to do for the assignment</span></li>
        <li aria-level="1"><span>Your expected timeline for completing the work</span></li>
        <li aria-level="1"><span>If you would like to copy and paste the example here as a template please do so</span></li>
    </ol>
    <p><strong>**You must include a link in order for your submission to work. Feel free to use www.google.com.</strong></p>
    <p><span>For example:</span></p>
    <p><span> “The ________ project is not complete because _________. I have ________, ________, and ________ complete but still need more time to complete _________, _________, and _________. I estimate that I will have this done by _________________.”</span></p>
</details>

 

Canvas integrates Bootstrap 3

Documentation: https://getbootstrap.com/docs/3.3/css/#tables -- table formatting

Some, but not all, of Bootstrap 3's styles are compatible with Canvas. You will have to try them out. In general, you'll find the table formatting is good, as well as some of the color styles.

wiki_pages.title: Creating and Configuring Your Notion Workspace
wiki_pages.body_html:
• Visit Notion and create an account using your AC email.

  • Now visit this page:https://anniecannons.notion.site/TEMPLATE-Your-name-s-Dashboard-12abb7044bb180c3b310ebaad7a196aa 
    • In the upper right corner of the page, click the Duplicate button. Duplicate this page to your own Notion workspace.

  • Your instructor will walk you through making customizations to your Notion workspace.

  • Share your Notion workspace with robyn@anniecannons.com, dawn@anniecannons.com, and your instructor(s) and/or TA, so they may see your work.

wiki_pages.title: CodePen Configuration and Your First Fork
wiki_pages.body_html:
We will be working with CodePen, a simple platform that allows students to concentrate on learning HTML, CSS, and JavaScript without worrying about web hosting, a development environment, or versioning.

Create a free CodePen account

Configure your CodePen environment

  • Put the code windows on the left via 3rd button from the right (choose code on left).
    Reason: This will make changing the screen size of your creation easier, helpful for debugging when we get to mobile things.

Under "Profile", this is where you can update your info: Photo, name, email, etc.

Under "Editor Settings," you can change your theme colors, fonts, and font sizes if you wish. While you are here, turn on Format On Save.

Forking in CodePen

Open your first CodePen: https://codepen.io/team/anniecannons/pen/OJYoOxG

Note the "fork" button in the lower right corner.

"Fork" means copy to your account. You will always fork the current pen before making changes.

Fork this CodePen to your account now. You will need to hit the Save button in the upper right corner to save your work.

In the HTML window, at the top of the code, type in your name or handle. Example:

Save your CodePen after you type in your name.

Copy the link at the top of your CodePen and turn this in to Canvas.
wiki_pages.manifest_title: CodePen Configuration and Your First Fork
